alkene.label: Alkene
alkene.common_mistake: Aromatic rings also contain C=C but behave very differently; do not call benzene an alkene.

alkyne.label: Alkyne
alkyne.common_mistake: Do not confuse the alkyne triple bond with a nitrile (C#N).

aromatic_ring.label: Aromatic ring
aromatic_ring.student_summary: A benzene-like aromatic ring of delocalised pi electrons.
aromatic_ring.common_mistake: Not every ring is aromatic; aromaticity needs a planar, fully conjugated cyclic system.

alkyl_halide.label: Alkyl halide
alkyl_halide.student_summary: A halogen attached to an sp3 carbon.
alkyl_halide.common_mistake: An aryl halide (halogen on an aromatic ring) is not an alkyl halide.

alcohol.label: Alcohol
alcohol.student_summary: An OH group attached to an sp3 carbon.
alcohol.common_mistake: An OH on an aromatic ring is a phenol, not an alcohol.

phenol.label: Phenol
phenol.student_summary: An OH group attached directly to an aromatic ring.
phenol.common_mistake: Phenols are more acidic than typical alcohols; do not treat them identically.

ether.label: Ether
ether.student_summary: An oxygen bonded to two carbon atoms.
ether.common_mistake: Esters also contain a C-O-C motif; check for the neighbouring carbonyl before calling it an ether.

epoxide.label: Epoxide
epoxide.student_summary: A strained three-membered ring containing one oxygen.
epoxide.common_mistake: Do not confuse an epoxide with a regular ether; ring strain drives its reactivity.

aldehyde.label: Aldehyde
aldehyde.student_summary: A carbonyl carbon bearing at least one hydrogen.
aldehyde.common_mistake: A ketone has two carbon substituents on the carbonyl; an aldehyde has a hydrogen.

ketone.label: Ketone
ketone.student_summary: A carbonyl carbon bonded to two other carbons.
ketone.common_mistake: Do not confuse with aldehydes (which carry an H on the carbonyl).

carboxylic_acid.label: Carboxylic acid
carboxylic_acid.student_summary: A carbonyl carbon bonded to an OH group.
carboxylic_acid.common_mistake: Do not confuse esters with carboxylic acids; esters have OR instead of OH.

ester.label: Ester
ester.student_summary: A carbonyl carbon bonded to an oxygen that links to another carbon.
ester.common_mistake: The single-bonded oxygen connects to carbon (OR), not hydrogen (OH).

amide.label: Amide
amide.student_summary: A carbonyl carbon bonded to a nitrogen.
amide.common_mistake: An amide nitrogen is far less basic than an amine nitrogen.

amine.label: Amine
amine.student_summary: A nitrogen bonded to carbons and/or hydrogens, not part of an amide.
amine.common_mistake: Nitrogen in an amide is not an amine even though both contain N.

nitrile.label: Nitrile
nitrile.common_mistake: Do not confuse the nitrile triple bond with a carbon-carbon alkyne.

thiol.label: Thiol
thiol.student_summary: A sulfur-hydrogen group, the sulfur analogue of an alcohol.
thiol.common_mistake: Thiols are more acidic and more nucleophilic than alcohols.

sulfide.label: Sulfide
sulfide.student_summary: A sulfur bonded to two carbons, the sulfur analogue of an ether.
sulfide.common_mistake: Do not confuse a sulfide with a thiol; a sulfide has no S-H bond.
